message:
test(packs/infrastructure): pattern coverage for ansible / pulumi / terraform

Adds unit tests inside the existing `mod tests` blocks for the three
infrastructure packs. No production patterns change in this commit —
each module's `create_destructive_patterns()` is unchanged. The tests
exercise the existing `assert_allows` / `assert_blocks` helpers from
`crate::packs::test_helpers` against representative command shapes.

src/packs/infrastructure/ansible.rs:
- `ansible_safe_dry_run_modes`: `--check`, `--diff`, `--list-hosts`,
  `--list-tasks`, `--syntax-check` should all be allowed for both
  `ansible` and `ansible-playbook`.
- `ansible_safe_info_commands`: `ansible-inventory --list`,
  `ansible-doc file`, `ansible-config dump` are read-only and allowed.
- `ansible_blocks_shell_rm_rf`: `ansible all -m shell -a 'rm -rf ...'`
  and the `command` module variant are blocked, with the matched
  reason carrying `rm -rf`.
- Additional cases: blocking shell-driven reboot/shutdown, etc.

src/packs/infrastructure/pulumi.rs:
- New tests under the existing `mod tests`, using the new
  `crate::packs::Severity` import to exercise severity-aware
  assertions.

src/packs/infrastructure/terraform.rs:
- Same pattern as pulumi: `Severity` is now imported in the test
  module and used to verify severity tagging on the matches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
